We're pausing the line to pass accumulation. Dakota Adair Curt Willis
The accumulation is caused by problems with the capper jamming when it is overfed.

There's actually two major causes of the accumulation. One being the over-feeding of jars through the machines. The other being lids which aren't fully secured on the jars. I'm currently working on coaching the team on how to avoid these issues.
We're restarting the line now.

MFM visual inspection, date change for product item 30906 from 04/06/2027 to 04/07/2027

On the MFM line, two deviations from established requirements and Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP) were identified: the presence of a waste container without a lid—leaving waste material exposed—and the use of gray scrap trays resting on cardboard instead of the authorized yellow support trays. These conditions had been reported previously, and second-shift personnel had taken corrective measures; however, correction was required again this afternoon. The team leader has been notified, and appropriate action has been taken.

The MFM line is operating within established parameters, with no deviations or incidents. All checks performed have yielded favorable results, demonstrating stable performance that meets established quality standards.
La maquina MFM esta presentando problemas constantemente debido a que no desliza correctamente por la textura de la bolsa
